metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1167
metadata.comment: Rationale: In this study, Egaña et al. demonstrated that Synaptgyrin 3 interacts with the dopamine transporter (DAT) using the split-ubiquitin system (Fig. 1), immunoprecipitation (Fig. 2), immunocytochemistry (Fig. 3) and FRET (Fig. 4). The authors also show that the N-terminal part of DAT can bind to synaptic vesicles (Fig. 6). Then they used a dopamine uptake assay and showed that overexpression of synaptogyrin 3 increases dopamine uptake in MN9D cells (Fig. 7) and PC12 cells (Fig 8). Intriguingly, the increase of cellular dopamine uptake caused by synaptogyrin 3 is dependent on VMAT activity. Since in the presence of the VMAT inhibitor, no increase in dopamine uptake was observed, synaptogyrin 3 is suggested to link dopaminergic synaptic vesicle to the plasma membrane dopamine transporter, thus funneling dopamine sequestered from the extracellular space directly into synaptic vesicles.. Experimental description: PC12 cells were cultured in DMEM supplemented with 5% FBS, 5% horse serum, 1 mm glutamine, and 50 μg/ml each penicillin and streptomycin at 37°C in a humidified, 10% CO2 incubator and transfected using Lipofectamine 2000 (Invitrogen).
